label: Naypyidaw
sub: SAC Junta Capital — Military administrative center; site of SAC command; largely isolated from conflict

label: Yangon
sub: Myanmar's largest city — CDM epicenter 2021; mass protests; continued SAC control; economic deterioration

label: Mandalay
sub: Myanmar's second city — Earthquake devastation (Mar 2025); PDF pressure in surrounding townships; SAC still holds city center

label: Lashio
sub: Northern Shan State capital — Captured by MNDAA/TNLA Aug 2024; first Regional Military Command HQ lost by junta

label: Laukkai
sub: Kokang capital — Captured by MNDAA Jan 5, 2024; thousands of online scam compound victims freed

label: Chin Shwe Haw
sub: Strategic Myanmar-China border crossing — Captured by MNDAA Nov 2023; cut SAC border revenue and supply routes

label: Sittwe
sub: Rakhine State capital — Under AA siege; one of last SAC holdouts in Rakhine; humanitarian crisis for trapped civilians

label: Myitkyina
sub: Kachin State capital — KIA pressing SAC outskirts; SAC increasingly isolated; major humanitarian crisis in region

label: Loikaw
sub: Kayah State capital — Karenni forces have partially surrounded city; SAC relies on airlifts; civilians trapped

label: Myawaddy
sub: Karen State border town — KNLA briefly captured Apr 2024; strategic trade corridor with Thailand; contested

label: Hpa-An
sub: Karen State capital — KNU/KNLA operations in surrounding areas; SAC under pressure; active frontlines nearby

label: Ann Township
sub: Former Western Regional Military Command HQ — Captured by AA Dec 20, 2024; devastating strategic loss for junta

label: Maungdaw
sub: Northern Rakhine — Captured by AA Dec 2024; AA now controls full 270-km Bangladesh border; Rohingya crisis epicenter

label: Mrauk-U
sub: Ancient Rakhine capital — AA captured entire district by Feb 2024; UNESCO heritage sites at risk during conflict

label: Paletwa
sub: Chin State — AA captured Jan 2024; controls Kaladan River corridor linking to India's transit project

label: Sagaing Region
sub: PDF heartland — Most villages burned campaign; highest number of airstrike incidents; over 800 villages burned by SAC

label: Kalay
sub: Sagaing Region — Chin-Sagaing border PDF stronghold; major PDF operations base; CDF and Sagaing PDF cooperation

label: Hakha
sub: Chin State capital — CDF controls surrounding areas; SAC garrison largely isolated; active resistance operations

label: Depayin (Kanbalu)
sub: SAC airstrike Oct 23, 2022 — Bombed charity concert killing 80+ civilians; one of deadliest single air attacks

label: Pazigyi Village
sub: SAC airstrike Apr 11, 2023 — 170+ killed at inauguration ceremony; UN Special Rapporteur called it 'act of pure terror'

label: Hpruso
sub: Christmas Eve Massacre Dec 24, 2021 — SAC forces burned 35+ civilians alive in vehicles; HRW documented as war crime

label: Lay Kay Kaw
sub: KNU liberated zone — Model resistance-administered town near Thai border; KNU provides civil governance and services

label: Mong Ko
sub: Northern Shan — Captured by MNDAA in early days of Op 1027; strategic border town controlling trade routes

label: Taunggyi
sub: Southern Shan State capital — SAC holdout but under pressure from RCSS/SSA and PDF forces in surrounding townships

label: Kyaukpyu
sub: Chinese-backed deep-sea port — One of last SAC holdouts in Rakhine; China concerned about project security; AA surrounds

label: Taungup
sub: Southern Rakhine — AA captured after 42-day siege Dec 2024; falls on key highway connecting Rakhine to Bago Region

label: Demoso
sub: Kayah State — Karenni resistance stronghold; KNDF/KA base; heavy SAC bombing of civilian areas documented

label: Bago
sub: Bago Region — Apr 9, 2021 massacre: SAC killed 80+ protesters; one of bloodiest single-day protests crackdowns

label: Let Yet Kone
sub: Kachin State — Apr 2022 airstrike on school/IDP camp killed 60+ civilians; UN called for accountability

label: Muse
sub: Northern Shan — Major China trade gateway; contested; Operation 1027 threatened key Muse-Mandalay highway trade
